lesson: When short ATR is depressed versus its longer baseline and RSI sits mid-zone without filter clearance, chop risk dominates; baseline gates withholding entry preserved capital.
Friendly EMA alignment with RSI not past the case threshold often resolves as mean drift inside a range; wait for stated extreme or volatility confirmation.
Compressed range under resistance with no expansion evidence produces fragile breakouts; expectancy improves only after confirmation data stated in the case.
